symmetry_types.name: Pentagonal / Icosahedral
symmetry_types.mathematical_basis: Projection from 4D (2D quasiperiodic) or 6D (3D icosahedral) hypercubic lattice onto quasiperiodic subspace
symmetry_types.explanation: 5-fold rotation is crystallographically forbidden. The interior angle of a regular pentagon (108°) cannot tile the plane by itself. Penrose showed that two tiles with related golden-ratio proportions tile aperiodically with global 5-fold symmetry.
symmetry_types.diffraction_signature: 10-fold diffraction pattern (5-fold rotational + inversion symmetry) with peak spacing ratios equal to φ = 1.618...
symmetry_types.historical_note: Forbidden symmetry observed by Shechtman in 1982, published 1984 — triggered redefinition of 'crystal' by IUCr in 1991

symmetry_types.name: Octagonal
symmetry_types.mathematical_basis: Projection from 4D hypercubic lattice Z^4 onto a 2D plane at angle related to √2; Ammann bars form a square-like grid
symmetry_types.explanation: 8-fold rotation axis is crystallographically forbidden. The Ammann-Beenker tiling uses squares and 45° rhombi with equal edge lengths and tiles quasi-periodically with 8-fold symmetry.
symmetry_types.diffraction_signature: 8-fold diffraction rings with peak spacing ratios involving 1+√2 = 2.414...
symmetry_types.historical_note: Ammann-Beenker tilings discovered independently by Robert Ammann and F. Beenker around 1982

symmetry_types.name: Decagonal
symmetry_types.point_group_3d: D10h (layered: 10-fold in plane, periodic along axis)
symmetry_types.mathematical_basis: 5-fold quasiperiodic tiling layers (Penrose-like) stacked periodically; projection from 5D lattice
symmetry_types.explanation: Decagonal quasicrystals are quasiperiodic in 2D (within layers) and periodic in the third dimension (stacking direction), giving 10-fold symmetry in diffraction when viewed along the periodic axis.
symmetry_types.diffraction_signature: 10-fold diffraction along axis direction; along stack direction — standard periodic (integer) diffraction
symmetry_types.historical_note: Discovered in Al-Mn-Pd by Tsai et al. 1989; Al-Co-Ni has been most widely studied

symmetry_types.name: Dodecagonal
symmetry_types.point_group_3d: D12h (layered: 12-fold in plane, periodic along axis)
symmetry_types.mathematical_basis: Mixed square-triangle tiling (Stampfli) from projection of 4D or 5D lattice; also related to modular arithmetic mod 12
symmetry_types.explanation: 12-fold symmetry is crystallographically forbidden. The Stampfli tiling uses three tile types (square, equilateral triangle, 30° rhombus) to fill the plane with 12-fold local symmetry.
symmetry_types.historical_note: First dodecagonal QC found in Cr-Ni thin films by Ishimasa et al. 1987; also found in soft matter (block copolymers, liquid crystals)

general_principles.crystallographic_restriction: Only 1, 2, 3, 4, 6-fold rotation axes are compatible with periodic lattice translation symmetry
general_principles.quasicrystal_definition_IUCr_1991: A material with a diffraction pattern with sharp peaks but no periodic translation symmetry
general_principles.higher_dimensional_description: All quasicrystals can be described as projections of higher-dimensional periodic lattices onto lower-dimensional quasiperiodic subspaces

metadata.description: Symmetry types relevant to quasicrystals and aperiodic tilings
